skill.name: circleci-automation
skill.description: Automate CircleCI tasks through Rube MCP, including pipeline triggers, workflow monitoring, job inspection, artifact lookup, and test metadata review.
skill.summary: Use Rube MCP to operate and inspect CircleCI pipelines, workflows, jobs, artifacts, and tests.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many apparent shell execution patterns, but these are markdown backticks around CircleCI and Rube MCP tool names, parameters, and examples, not executable Ruby or shell code. The confirmed risk is legitimate networked automation through Rube MCP and CircleCI, which can trigger CI pipelines and retrieve artifacts or test metadata when the user has authorized access.

medium_findings.title: Networked CI/CD Automation Through Third-Party MCP
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server and use CircleCI tools through Rube MCP. This is consistent with the skill purpose, but it can trigger pipelines and retrieve CI artifacts or test metadata after authorization, so users should understand the operational and data-access impact.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The MCP endpoint and CircleCI automation workflow are explicit in the skill. The behavior appears legitimate, but it involves authenticated external services and CI/CD operations.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Markdown References
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported many Ruby or shell backtick execution findings. Review found markdown inline code and fenced examples naming MCP tools, CircleCI parameters, IDs, and response fields, with no script body or executable shell invocation in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is visibly markdown formatting and documentation. No evidence found of actual shell command execution or code that consumes user input.

low_findings.title: Static Blocker Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance findings do not correspond to cryptographic code, host inspection, or local reconnaissance behavior. The cited lines describe CircleCI metadata, workflow listing, rate limits, and quick-reference tool names.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptive markdown for CircleCI usage, not cryptographic primitives or system commands. No evidence found of local host enumeration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Authenticated External Service Operations
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill depends on Rube MCP and CircleCI authentication, then guides the assistant to trigger pipelines and inspect CI resources. This is not malicious, but it is a sensitive operational pattern because it changes CI state and accesses build data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The tool sequences are documented directly and require authenticated access. The risk is bounded to intended CircleCI automation rather than arbitrary command execution.

content.user_title: Automate CircleCI Pipelines with Rube MCP
content.value_statement: Manual CircleCI checks and reruns slow teams during releases and incident response. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Rube MCP workflows for pipeline, job, artifact, and test-result tasks.
actual_capabilities: Guides setup for a Rube MCP CircleCI connection.
Triggers CircleCI pipelines by project slug, branch, tag, or parameters.
Lists pipelines and workflows, including pagination guidance.
Retrieves CircleCI job details using project slug and job number.
Finds job artifacts and explains authentication considerations for downloads.
Reviews test metadata when jobs upload compatible test results.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP availability and an active CircleCI connection.
Does not include standalone scripts or direct CircleCI API client code.
Cannot validate pipeline parameters without the current project configuration.
Artifact and test metadata access depends on CircleCI permissions and job output.

use_cases.title: Release Pipeline Kickoff
use_cases.description: Trigger a CircleCI pipeline for a selected branch or tag and monitor the resulting workflows.

use_cases.title: Build Failure Triage
use_cases.description: Inspect recent pipelines, workflow status, job details, and uploaded test metadata after a failed build.

use_cases.title: Artifact Collection
use_cases.description: Locate build artifacts from completed CircleCI jobs and identify the job numbers needed for review.

prompt_templates.title: Check Connection
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the CircleCI automation skill to verify that Rube MCP is available and that my CircleCI connection is active.

prompt_templates.title: Trigger Branch Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Trigger a CircleCI pipeline for project gh/my-org/my-repo on branch main, then show the workflow status.

prompt_templates.title: Investigate Failed Job
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the latest failed pipeline for gh/my-org/my-repo on branch release, list workflows, and inspect the failed job details.

prompt_templates.title: Review Artifacts and Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: For job number 123 in gh/my-org/my-repo, retrieve artifact information and summarize available test metadata with failures first.

output_examples.input: Check the latest pipeline status for gh/acme/api on main.
output: Confirms the CircleCI connection before querying project data.
Lists recent pipelines for the main branch and identifies the newest pipeline.
Summarizes workflow names, states, and any jobs that need attention.

output_examples.input: Trigger a release pipeline for tag v2.4.0.
output: Validates the project slug and tag-based trigger request.
Starts the CircleCI pipeline through the authenticated MCP tool.
Returns the pipeline identifier and explains how workflow status will be checked.
Retrieves job details to confirm the job ran and uploaded test results.
Reviews test metadata and groups failed tests before passing tests.
Notes when no test metadata is available from the CircleCI job.

best_practices: Confirm the active CircleCI connection before triggering or querying project data.
Use exact project slugs and verify whether the request uses a branch or a tag.
Poll workflows at reasonable intervals to avoid CircleCI rate limits.
anti_patterns: Do not provide both branch and tag when triggering one pipeline.
Do not treat pipeline IDs, workflow IDs, and job numbers as interchangeable.
Do not assume artifacts or test metadata exist before confirming job completion.

faq.question: Does this skill require CircleCI credentials?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires an active CircleCI connection through Rube MCP before it can perform authenticated actions.

faq.question: Can it trigger new CircleCI pipelines?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides guidance for triggering pipelines with project slugs, branches, tags, and parameters.

faq.question: Can it download artifacts directly?
faq.answer: It guides artifact lookup through CircleCI tools. Actual download access depends on authentication and artifact permissions.

faq.question: Does it include executable scripts?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a markdown guide for MCP tool usage and does not contain local automation scripts.

faq.question: Why should tool schemas be searched first?
faq.answer: Rube MCP tool schemas can change. Searching first helps the assistant use current parameters and response formats.

faq.question: What should users verify before publishing this skill internally?
faq.answer: Users should verify CircleCI permissions, approved MCP endpoints, and policies for triggering CI jobs from assistants.
